body_html:
go aheaed

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

The open cockpit is the rear area behind the cabin,
or aft deck. Do Craig's
recommendations meet with your approval. May I tell
him to get the quote
based on these recommendations.

Darren

From: "Craig" Craig@penumbramarine.com
To: DKIESQ@aol.com
Subject: FW: Fwd: 41 Cabin
Date: Fri, 10 Mar 2006 18:06:57 -0500

Sorry - I opened your attachment and replied to it.
I see it was JEE that
received it.

Regards,

Craig Tafoya

Penumbra Marine Logistics
(954) 791-9992 Office
(954) 328-6203 Mobile

-----Original Message-----
From: Craig [mailto:Craig@penumbramarine.com]
Sent: Friday, March 10, 2006 3:25 PM
To: 'J. Epstein'
Subject: RE: Fwd: 41 Cabin

The open cockpit is the area behind the cabin, or
aft deck.

Regards,

Craig Tafoya

Penumbra Marine Logistics
(954) 791-9992 Office
(954) 328-6203 Mobile

-----Original Message-----
From: J. Epstein [mailto:jeeproject@yahoo.com]
Sent: Friday, March 10, 2006 2:30 PM
To: DKIESQ@aol.com
Subject: Re: Fwd: 41 Cabin

what does open rear cockpit mean?

--- DKIESQ@aol.com wrote:

This is Craig's final recommendation after
discussing matters with Miles and
me and questioning Nautica. As to the Arch.
Craig

said that there is no
variety of arches offered. You can either get
the

one offered by Nautica or
not. Craig said that of course Nautica could
custom build anything you want,
but Craig did not feel that was necessary.

There is no standard bathroom so we eliminated
that

option.

I have copied miles on this list of options and
with

your approval I will
get a final quote on these from Craig/Nautica.

Please let me know.

Darren

From: "Craig" Craig@penumbramarine.com
To: DKIEsq@aol.com
Subject: 41 Cabin
Date: Fri, 10 Mar 2006 13:11:11 -0500

Darren,

Please find the following summary and
suggestions

for the Nautica 41 cabin.

  • 41' cabin RIB with the cabin made of Kevlar
    for

weight restrictions

  • Color of deck to be white. Cabin color TBD
    at a

later date

  • Tube color TBD at a later date

  • Two 300 gallon fuel tanks

  • Open rear cockpit with storage box

  • Cockpit lighting for deck and cabin area

  • Watertight door (aluminum) to cockpit

  • 2 Swivel captain's chairs by Stidd

  • Sliding glass windows on sides (spray water
    tight)

  • Windshield wipers

  • Forward cabin V-berth seating / cushions

  • Storage locker down below

  • VHF radio- ICOM

  • Air Conditioning / Polar Air

  • Mase Diesel Generator of 6.6kw (I am
    checking

to see if a separate
diesel tank is included and if it is in addition
to

the 300 gallons of
gasoline) Worst case would be to install a gas
powered generator.

  • Night Vision by Raytheon
  • Bow lights (set)- Facing forward from top of
    superstructure to
    illuminate the bow area and any close objects or
    docks
  • Underwater lights - Suggest these to be
    located

aft as the cockpit
will be the most used area. These will also
illuminate the area below the
props and shine away from any beach rather than
into

the sand on the forward
area.

  • Courtesy lights in cockpit area
  • Small inexpensive digital depth sounder as
    backup
  • Garmin 198C GPS plotter/sounder
  • Bow Step with foldable cleat
  • 2 remote controlled spot lights (1 forward
    and 1

aft)

  • RayMarine Radar RL 70C
  • AM/FM Stereo CD with 2 speakers
  • 8 SOLAS approved Life Jackets
  • Anchor windless
  • Police Light Bar with Siren
  • Police Blue Light
  • White powder coated arch to fit additional
    Police lights, etc.

Regards,

Craig Tafoya

Penumbra Marine Logistics

(954) 791-9992 Office

(954) 328-6203 Mobile
